catalysts: Q2 2026 earnings (late July) — decisive proof of realized pricing capture

V1.name: Fundamentals Trajectory
V1.rationale: **UPGRADED 2026-03-21 (4→5).** S3 pricing confirmed: NOLA physical $673-675/st ($742-745/t equiv) sustained above $636/st trigger. EBITDA inflecting from $808M toward $1,100-1,700M at S3 pricing. Gas cost moat widened to $13+/MMBtu vs Europe (HH $2.92 vs TTF $16.7). 97% utilization at nameplate ~10.4M tons. Revenue/earnings growth accelerating. Margin expansion visible and confirmed by commodity pricing. Management alignment: $4.78B buybacks over 4 years (95% of FCF returned); DNB-analog constraint via capital return discipline. Insider ownership 0.53% is low but offset by buyback commitment and structural gas cost advantage. (Sources: Argus Mar 18-19; EIA; Q4 2025 call; 10-K FY2025)

V2.name: Valuation Mismatch
V2.rationale: **DOWNGRADED 2026-04-05 (5→4). [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.4, 2026-04-05] Council rescore: equity +59% YTD, above ALL analyst PTs including UBS $140. BofA explicitly argues benefits reflected. Market HAS partially priced thesis — event/momentum buyers embedded war premium even though sell-side consensus frozen at $9.01. No longer "zero credit" per 5/5 rubric.** Prior: April Tampa settlement confirmed at ~$775/mt CFR (+$160/mt from March $615/mt), breaching the pre-defined >$700 S3 confirmation threshold. Physical NOLA urea $673–675/st ($742–745/t equiv) sustained above $636/st trigger. Both primary commodity benchmarks (urea + ammonia) now confirm S3 pricing. Equity mismatch is extreme: sell-side consensus EPS frozen at $7.65 — gives **zero credit** to S3/S4 scenarios. Prob-weighted EPS ~$30.7 (updated for Tampa) implies P/E ~4.0x at $122. Historical peak-cycle range 5.5-7x. Agricultural cascade (corn planting reduction at $700/t urea) not yet reflected in equity valuations. UBS $140 PT (Mar 26) is first broker within range but still only 5.3x on S3 EPS — consensus has not moved. Meets 5/5: "Market gives zero credit for the thesis. Consensus estimates reflect no awareness of the catalyst or trajectory change." *(Sources: Argus Tampa April settlement ~$775/mt; NOLA physical barge Mar 18-19; CBOT JCH26; CHANGELOG Mar 19, Mar 30)*

V3.name: Identifiable Mispricing Reason
V3.rationale: **UPGRADED 2026-03-24 (4→5).** Triple supply constraint removes the market's last expected safety valve. Mispricing reason now fully specific, verifiable, and demonstrably temporary: (1) No strategic fertilizer reserve — government cannot intervene (confirmed Mar 17, no mechanism exists); (2) **Chinese supply response foreclosed by policy (Mar 24)** — Beijing halted NK blend exports, reiterated urea restrictions, 40-75% of China's total fertilizer exports constrained through August minimum; (3) Hormuz physical blockade + Ras Laffan structural damage (QAFCO 5.6 Mt/y offline) = no ME supply pathway; (4) Self-correcting mechanism: time passing with no alternative supply source forces commodity repricing → equity repricing follows. Meets 5/5: "Mispricing reason specific, verifiable, demonstrably temporary." *(Sources: Intel Brief Mar 11 Evening; CHANGELOG Mar 15; CHANGELOG Mar 24 — China fertilizer restrictions)*

V4.name: Downside Protection
V4.rationale: **UPGRADED 2026-03-31 (3→4). Dual chokepoint reactivation bounds binary ceasefire risk.** Houthi formally entered war (Mar 28), threatening Bab el-Mandeb independently of Hormuz. Hormuz-only ceasefire no longer fully unwinds thesis — Red Sea independently hostile. Max headline drawdown reduced from 20-30% to 15-20% (only one of two chokepoints resolves). Business floor strong: $1.98B cash, no debt until 2034, $1.8B FCF, $2/share dividend, $1.7B buyback authorization. Mine deployment (Maham 3/7) creates physical disruption floor weeks-months post any ceasefire. Gas risk reduced: HH $2.92; LNG near-maxed. DOJ probe: civil class action (no grand jury evidence); 2008 precedent = manageable. Meets 4/5 rubric: "Limited failure modes. Binary risks bounded but with some uncertainty. Floor exists and is reasonably hard. Drawdown recoverable." *(Sources: CHANGELOG Mar 31 dual chokepoint entry; 10-K FY2025 p.68; NOTES.md Mar 25-26; QUESTIONS.md Mar 26)*

V5.name: Thesis Simplicity
V5.rationale: **Refreshed 2026-03-27.** Two conditions: (1) Hormuz remains closed — **HOLDING, Day 28. Physically reinforced by Maham 3/7 mine deployment (Mar 26). Zolghadr hardliner SNSC appointment. Iran 5-point counter demands Hormuz sovereignty (US non-starter). 5,000 Marines deploying. Both sides at maximalist positions.** Mine clearance weeks-months post any ceasefire = condition 1 physically locked. (2) Nat gas stays below $5.50 — **HOLDING at $2.92/MMBtu (Mar 24). Headroom: 85%.** EIA storage 1,883 Bcf, 47 Bcf above 5-yr avg. Both conditions holding with condition 1 now physically reinforced. *(Sources: THESIS.md Mar 26; NOTES.md Mar 25)*

V6.name: Upside Optionality
V6.rationale: **UPGRADED 2026-03-30 (4→5). Tampa confirmation de-weakens ammonia path; all 5 paths confirmed active.** Five paths: (1) S4 duration extension (29% probability) — EPS +$12-20; (2) India emergency procurement — gas cut 30%, IFFCO+3 FM, $28/MMBtu emergency LNG, domestic production +23% to 67,000 MT/day (demand NOT destroyed — India applying maximum supply levers); (3) Non-urea margin expansion — **ammonia path restored**: Tampa $775/mt CFR (+$160/mt) confirms export ammonia pricing diverged upward from domestic NOLA weakness. Tampa is the benchmark for 30.7% of CF revenue; domestic NOLA ammonia softness is irrelevant to CF's export-weighted ammonia margins. UAN/AN intact; (4) buyback acceleration on elevated FCF ($4-5B S3 FCF vs $1.3B FY2025 pace); (5) Blue Point/low-carbon re-rating ($3B mid-cycle EBITDA by 2030, CBAM premiums $100+/ton). All 5 paths confirmed active with no weakened sub-components. Meets 5/5: "5+ independent ways to win. Base case is conservative; multiple scenarios deliver outsized returns." *(Sources: Tampa April settlement ~$775/mt; NOTES.md Mar 23, Mar 30; 10-K FY2025 Note 4; Q4 2025 call)*

V7.name: Evidence Quality
V7.rationale: Core pillars [FACT]-sourced: cost structure (10-K audited), supply disruption magnitude (management + shipping data), urea pricing (observable market — NOLA physical $673-675/st confirmed Day 20), Indian cascade (force majeures confirmed), Polska Azoty halt (European cost squeeze). Inferential elements: S3 probability (60%), EPS accretion model ($24-33), nat gas staying below threshold. Several QUESTIONS.md items resolved since Mar 15 (gas seasonal confirmed, spot-to-realized lag quantified, demand destruction threshold resolved). Research Confidence 4/5 (upgraded Mar 17). *(Sources: 10-K FY2025; 9 transcripts reviewed; QUESTIONS.md; NOTES.md Mar 17)*

V8.name: Catalyst Clarity
V8.rationale: **DOWNGRADED 2026-04-05 (5→4). [COUNCIL: all, gpt-5.4, 2026-04-05] Council rescore: Q1 earnings (May 6) only partially captures March spike due to 10-20% spot-to-realized lag — March pricing flows primarily into Q2. Q1 consensus at $2.39 means even a beat may underwhelm vs war narrative. Decisive forcing function is Q2 (late July), not Q1 — creates timing uncertainty that distinguishes 4/5 from 5/5.** Prior: (1) Trump Mar 28 deadline expiry (binary escalation/capitulation); (2) USDA Prospective Plantings Mar 31 (consensus 94-95M corn acres, >92M demand threshold); (3) April Tampa ammonia settlement (imminent, first war-conditions NH3 benchmark, V1/V6 trigger at >$700). Plus: Q1 earnings May 6 (consensus $2.19, likely significant beat); India Kharif Apr-Jun; mine clearance timeline as new physically-grounded forcing function. All dated, observable, independently price-moving. *(Sources: NOTES.md Mar 23-26; CF IR calendar; QUESTIONS.md Mar 26)*

blindspots: **Yazoo product mix granularity** — CF's Yazoo City complex produces UAN, DEF, and other products alongside urea. Exact mix not disclosed quarterly. Matters because UAN and DEF have different price dynamics than granular urea. Proxy: segment revenue per ton implied from quarterly disclosure.

**Real-time Chinese coal-to-methanol/urea restart pace** — Argus Media data lags 2-4 weeks. Chinese producers announced 3.3 MMT restarts but POSTPONED pending ceasefire clarity. Actual restart pace determines how quickly supply responds if Hormuz reopens. No real-time tracking available. Monitor via: monthly Argus/ICIS China fertilizer reports.

**India government fertilizer subsidy budget ceiling** — India subsidizes fertilizer imports. If the budget ceiling is hit, import volumes cap regardless of farmer demand. Budget details are opaque and politically sensitive. Proxy: track IFFCO import tender volumes and government procurement announcements.

**US inland logistics capacity** — CF ships by barge on the Mississippi. Spring flood/drought conditions affect logistics and pricing spreads (NOLA vs. inland). Not trackable in advance; emerges as USACE lock reports.

**Post-Hormuz mine clearance timeline** — No historical precedent at this scale in a major shipping lane. US Navy estimates "weeks to months." This is the physical floor on disruption duration regardless of diplomatic outcome. Monitor via: NAVCENT/C5F public advisories.

noiseFilter: **Daily urea spot price swings (<5%)** — NOLA barge market trades on thin volume. Day-to-day moves of $10-20/st are noise. Only weekly trends matter.

**Analyst rating changes without new modeling** — Most sell-side analysts haven't published Hormuz-scenario models. A "downgrade" based on pre-Hormuz numbers is meaningless. Only react to analysts who publish updated scenario analysis.

**Weather-related planting delays (<2 weeks)** — Normal spring variation. Only delays >3 weeks that compress the application window are material (force lower-quality substitution or reduced total application).

**Ceasefire rumors without operational confirmation** — Witkoff/Kushner back-channel generates headlines. Until insurance underwriters resume transit coverage AND mine clearance begins, physical disruption persists regardless of diplomatic statements. Test: Lloyd's List shipping traffic data, not Reuters headlines.
**CF share price moves on market-wide selloffs** — CF correlates with energy/materials in risk-off. If urea prices hold while CF drops, that's a buying opportunity, not a thesis challenge.

structural.current: **De facto comprehensive fertilizer export ban** (Reuters, 5 industry sources, Mar 18-20). Only ammonium sulphate exportable. Not formally published by MOFCOM but operationally enforced. Half to three-quarters of China's $13B annual exports (~40 MMT) restricted. Expected to persist **until at least August 2026.** India formally requested China issue urea export quotas due to Hormuz supply disruption.

structural.threshold: Lifting restrictions = supply valve reopens (S1/S2 reversion); Extension past Aug = S3 strengthened
